description: prepare-save-request.ts:47-48 accesses Timestamp and Frontmatter internals via unsafe casts: `(input.note.frontmatter.createdAt as any).epochMillis` and `(now as any).epochMillis`. The Timestamp type is opaque by design (per value-objects.ts). This breaks the abstraction boundary and couples the validation logic to the internal representation of Timestamp. If the Timestamp representation changes (e.g., to an ISO string or a branded number), this code silently breaks at runtime with no compile-time error. Similarly, serialize-note.ts:22-24 and :42 use `(ts as { epochMillis: number }).epochMillis` casts.

evidence.snippet:
const createdAtMs = (input.note.frontmatter.createdAt as any).epochMillis;
const nowMs = (now as any).epochMillis;
